title: URL Shortener
description: Design a scalable URL shortening service like bit.ly that handles billions of redirects per day with low latency.
requirements: Given a long URL, return a short URL (6-8 char alias)
Redirect short URL to original long URL
URLs should expire after a configurable TTL (default 1 year)
Track click analytics per short URL

components.label: Client
components.javaNote: Browser or mobile client. Sends POST /shorten with long URL in body. Receives 201 + short URL in response.

components.label: API Gateway
components.javaNote: Spring Cloud Gateway or AWS API Gateway. Handles rate limiting, auth, and routes /shorten to Shortener Service and /:alias to Redirect Service.

components.label: Shortener Svc
components.javaNote: Spring Boot service. Calls ID Generator, encodes to base62, stores mapping in Postgres. Base62: chars 0-9A-Za-z → 62^6 ≈ 56 billion unique aliases.

components.label:
ID Generator
(Snowflake)
components.javaNote: Twitter Snowflake-style 64-bit ID: 41-bit timestamp + 10-bit machine ID + 12-bit sequence. Guarantees unique monotonic IDs without coordination. Base62 encode lower 38 bits → 6 char alias.

components.label: Cache (Redis)
components.javaNote: Redis stores alias→URL mappings with TTL. On redirect: GET alias → if hit, return 301; if miss, query DB, store in Redis with remaining TTL. Cache hit ratio target: 99%.

components.label: DB (Postgres)
components.javaNote: Stores: (alias VARCHAR PK, long_url TEXT, created_at, expires_at, user_id). Sharded by alias hash. Index on created_at for expiry cleanup job. 301 vs 302: use 301 (permanent) to let browser cache redirect; use 302 if you need accurate click counting.

components.label:
Analytics Queue
(Kafka)
components.javaNote: Redirect service publishes ClickEvent{alias, timestamp, userAgent, ip} to Kafka. Analytics consumers aggregate asynchronously — keeps redirect latency unaffected by analytics writes.

componentBreakdown.heading: Snowflake ID → Base62 Encoding
componentBreakdown.detail: Generate globally unique IDs without distributed coordination. Encode to base62 for URL-safe short aliases.

componentBreakdown.heading: Redis Cache-Aside Pattern
componentBreakdown.detail: Read from cache first; on miss, load from DB and populate cache with TTL. 301 redirects let browsers cache the mapping client-side for maximum throughput.

componentBreakdown.heading: Write Path — Shortener Service
componentBreakdown.detail: Accepts long URL, generates alias, persists to Postgres. Handles custom aliases with uniqueness check. Publishes creation event for analytics.
